jokes.text: Very inspirational speech, ma'am. Come on, you wrote it, you can't review it.
jokes.setup: Assistant praising Selina's speech delivery
jokes.punchline: Selina's dismissive response reveals she knows it was generic because she wrote it herself
jokes.explanation: Strong character comedy showing Selina's self-aware narcissism. Economical delivery with good structure.

jokes.text: This is what DC must have been like under Jefferson... except that would have been a horse, right?
jokes.setup: Comparing Silicon Valley's innovation to Jefferson's DC
jokes.punchline: The anachronistic realization that Jefferson would have been on horseback
jokes.explanation: Clever historical observation with good absurdist logic, though not deeply character-specific.

jokes.text: She might have a 'great sense of density' for this nation
jokes.setup: Website typo discussion
jokes.punchline: Accidentally calling the VP dense instead of having 'destiny'
jokes.explanation: Classic typo humor that works for the political satire context, though fairly predictable.

jokes.text: 20,000 and Selina sits on your lap. - What did you just say? - No, not really.
jokes.setup: Escalating fundraising ticket prices
jokes.punchline: Dan's inappropriate suggestion followed by immediate backpedaling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan character moment - inappropriate comment with instant regret. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Gary, what are you doing? You look like a newborn giraffe.
jokes.setup: Gary doing something awkward with his movements
jokes.punchline: Selina's specific and vivid animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent Selina insult with vivid, specific imagery. Perfect character integration and highly quotable.

jokes.text: I was just fanning myself with my arm.
jokes.setup: Gary explaining his awkward movements
jokes.punchline: The absurd simplicity of his explanation for looking ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Good Gary character moment showing his earnest explanation for being ridiculous.

jokes.text: I call that an 'ussy.'
jokes.setup: Someone suggests taking a selfie
jokes.punchline: Selina's attempt at hip slang that sounds sexual
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina trying to be cool and failing spectacularly. The accidental sexual innuendo is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: - She's nine months. - Okay, she's rounding up.
jokes.setup: Mother correcting baby's age
jokes.punchline: Selina treating baby development like business metrics
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Selina's political mind - even baby ages get spun. Excellent economy and character integration.

jokes.text: They've been fracking in our area.
jokes.setup: Baby being slow to develop
jokes.punchline: Blaming it on hydraulic fracturing
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic connecting unrelated environmental concerns to baby development.

jokes.text: We need to make that woman go away. - And I don't mean kill her. - Kill her.
jokes.setup: Amy clarifying she doesn't mean violence
jokes.punchline: Dan and Selina immediately suggesting exactly that
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy escalation showing the characters' ruthless political instincts. Excellent timing and economy.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I've got the baby!
jokes.setup: Political conversation getting heated
jokes.punchline: Selina realizes she's still holding the baby during the argument
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy moment showing how politicians get distracted from human interactions by politics.

jokes.text: She was like a beached whale with one flipper fucking around in the air, and you were nowhere to push her back in the sea.
jokes.setup: Dan critiquing Amy's handling of the situation
jokes.punchline: Elaborate and cruel whale metaphor for Selina's struggle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dan insult with vivid imagery. The extended metaphor is both cruel and precisely visual. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: That baby had a mean look in its eyes, like its mama.
jokes.setup: Dan explaining why he avoided the baby situation
jokes.punchline: Attributing malice to an infant because of genetics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan character moment - finding a way to insult both mother and baby. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: I'm calling this 'Pacific Trim.' - You know what 'trim' is? - Yeah? - It's twat.
jokes.setup: Mike suggesting a West Coast theme name
jokes.punchline: Selina pointing out the sexual slang meaning
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Mike's obliviousness to sexual innuendo and Selina's blunt correction.

jokes.text: Remember, while you're there, do not use 'Google' as a verb. They 'Yahoo' things there.
jokes.setup: Briefing Selina on tech company etiquette
jokes.punchline: The absurd corporate loyalty requirement to use competitor's name as verb
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of tech company tribalism and the absurdity of corporate loyalty extending to language.

jokes.text: I lose women and what am I left with? I'm left with gay Latinos and Jews at college, I guess.
jokes.setup: Selina worried about losing women voters
jokes.punchline: Her reductive view of her remaining coalition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy showing Selina's cynical demographic calculations and casual reductionism.

jokes.text: I have to succeed where the Republican Guard failed.
jokes.setup: Selina talking about taking out Danny Chung
jokes.punchline: Comparing her political opposition to military warfare against Saddam's forces
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy comparing political rivalry to military conflict. Perfect Selina grandiosity and historical reference.

jokes.text: You know, they say all babies are cute, but whoever 'they' are should be stuck in a stroller and pushed into traffic.
jokes.setup: General comment about baby cuteness
jokes.punchline: Amy's violent imagery about people who say babies are cute
jokes.explanation: Excellent Amy character moment - taking a mundane saying to a dark, violent conclusion. Strong structure and imagery.

jokes.text: So, the Veep's gone to Silicon Valley of the Dolls. And instead of California dreaming, she's got 'em California screaming.
jokes.setup: Jonah's blog post about Selina's trip
jokes.punchline: Elaborate pun combining movie reference with California song reference
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah overwrought wordplay. Shows his character but the puns are trying too hard.

jokes.text: 'But I've been drinking that frack water, and look what it's gone done to my titty milk!' 'My baby, oh, my baby!'
jokes.setup: Jonah doing voices for his blog post
jokes.punchline: His crude imitation of the concerned mother
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's crude sensibility and lack of empathy, but the humor itself is fairly cheap shock value.

jokes.text: Sue. Sue-ster. Sue of steel. Sue-Sue-Sue-dio.
jokes.setup: Jonah calling Sue
jokes.punchline: His stream of increasingly desperate Sue puns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah character moment showing his annoying habit of over-punning names. The escalation works well.

jokes.text: Jonah, get off the line. And then the planet.
jokes.setup: Sue telling Jonah to get off the phone
jokes.punchline: Adding that he should also get off Earth entirely
jokes.explanation: Excellent Sue line with perfect escalation and economy. The deadpan delivery of wanting him off the planet is perfect.

jokes.text: Dan-zibar, what's happening?
jokes.setup: Kent calling Dan
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at casual nickname creation
jokes.explanation: Good Kent character moment showing his awkward attempts at being casual and cool.

jokes.text: He just swallowed his phone in fear.
jokes.setup: Kent insulting Jonah over the phone
jokes.punchline: Kent's description of Jonah's terrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Great visual imagery of extreme fear reaction. Good economy and vivid absurd image.

jokes.text: I haven't seen my penis since the first Gulf War, and I kinda miss the little guy.
jokes.setup: Ben talking about getting out of shape on the job
jokes.punchline: His nostalgic reference to his hidden genitals
jokes.explanation: Classic Ben crude humor with perfect character integration. The 'little guy' endearment makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Well, seems his unit in Iraq did that to some poor slob with a drill.
jokes.setup: Ben mentioning Chung's Iraq service
jokes.punchline: Casually describing alleged torture with power tools
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Ben's characteristic understatement. The casual tone makes the content more shocking.

jokes.text: It's still Tuesday for another six minutes.
jokes.setup: Ordering whiskey chasers
jokes.punchline: Ben's technical justification for day-drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben logic and character consistency. The specificity of timing makes the rationalization funnier.

jokes.text: Mike, I am balls deep in this omelet.
jokes.setup: Mike busy with breakfast
jokes.punchline: Applying sexual metaphor to breakfast preparation
jokes.explanation: Great Mike character moment showing his oblivious use of inappropriate metaphors for mundane activities.

jokes.text: No, you shouldn't make your first million until you're in your 30s. That's what Andrew and I did. It kept us completely grounded.
jokes.setup: Selina commenting on young wealth
jokes.punchline: Claiming making millions in your 30s keeps you 'grounded'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina obliviousness to her own privilege. The irony of calling millionaire status 'grounded' is expertly delivered.

jokes.text: Poor kids, they don't realize they're all gonna be executed by the time they turn 30.
jokes.setup: Looking at young tech workers
jokes.punchline: Selina's casual prediction of mass execution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy showing Selina's cynical worldview. The casual tone makes the extreme prediction funnier.

jokes.text: I am the oldest person at Clovis by five years. I feel ancient. - You must be 12 years old, then.
jokes.setup: Melissa complaining about feeling old
jokes.punchline: Selina's sarcastic math about her actual age
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline structure showing Selina's impatience with millennial complaints about aging.

jokes.text: Absolutely, although it is pronounced 'Cray-eeg.' - 'Creg.' - 'Cray-eeg.' - 'Cray-eeg.' Very close. - 'Cray-eeg.' - That's it. Well done, Amy.
jokes.setup: Amy struggling to pronounce Craig's pretentious name pronunciation
jokes.punchline: The painful back-and-forth with condescending praise
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy about pretentious name pronunciation. Sets up running gag that pays off throughout.

jokes.text: Oh. You got it right, 'Ah-mee.'
jokes.setup: After correcting Amy's pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Melissa passive-aggressively mispronouncing Amy's simple name
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and callback. The passive-aggressive pronunciation of a simple name is perfectly petty.

jokes.text: We could 'build' a consensus.
jokes.setup: Kent seeing Legos in the office
jokes.punchline: His terrible pun about building consensus with building blocks
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Kent pun that shows his character but isn't actually funny - which is the point.

jokes.text: My brain feels like it's being fucking circumcised.
jokes.setup: Dan suffering from hangover
jokes.punchline: His vivid and disturbing metaphor for brain pain
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Dan metaphor combining pain with sexual imagery in a uniquely disturbing way. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: Get me a cheeseburger made of aspirin.
jokes.setup: Dan's hangover needs
jokes.punchline: Combining food craving with medication need in absurd way
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor showing Dan's desperate state. The specific food/medicine combo is perfectly weird.

jokes.text: You're gonna sit there and piss your pants like a man while I take your money.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining poker rules
jokes.punchline: Combining masculine posturing with infantile behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben character line combining macho poker talk with absurd imagery. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: I got a hangover worse than the film they made after 'The Hangover.'
jokes.setup: Dan describing his hangover severity
jokes.punchline: Using bad movie sequels as pain comparison
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture comparison that works for Dan's character, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: Craig's coding hour is sacred. It happens every day. but you don't know when.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Craig isn't available
jokes.punchline: The contradiction of daily but unpredictable 'sacred' time
jokes.explanation: Great satire of tech company mysticism and the absurd reverence for 'genius' schedules.

jokes.text: No, ma'am, that's Ron Jeremy. - I know, he's a great actor. He was Scar in 'The Lion King.' - That was Jeremy Irons. Ron Jeremy is a porn legend.
jokes.setup: Selina about to sign graffiti wall near porn star signature
jokes.punchline: Her complete confusion between Jeremy Irons and Ron Jeremy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Selina's pop culture blindness and the staff's horror at her near-mistake.

jokes.text: What are you doing? - It's like a rollaway. But there's no wheels.
jokes.setup: Gary struggling with some kind of furniture
jokes.punchline: His explanation reveals the fundamental flaw in his approach
jokes.explanation: Gary's logical thinking applied to illogical situation with furniture

jokes.text: Three pretty ladies, like Saturday night at Chez Jonah.
jokes.setup: Jonah winning at poker with three queens
jokes.punchline: Comparing his hand to his alleged dating life
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah bragging that reveals more about his insecurity than success. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Because unlike your boss, his day-to-fuck-up ratio isn't one to one.
jokes.setup: Discussing why no one goes after Chung
jokes.punchline: Ben's mathematical assessment of Selina's failure rate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ben line with perfect mathematical precision applied to political failure. Exceptionally quotable and economical.

jokes.text: Our withdrawal from Afghanistan has been more 'momentarily' than this.
jokes.setup: Waiting to meet Craig who will see them 'momentarily'
jokes.punchline: Comparing the wait to the endless Afghanistan withdrawal
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire with good timing. The comparison works on multiple levels and shows Kent's dry wit.

jokes.text: I shouldn't say this because I majored in math, but you have my 1000% attention.
jokes.setup: Craig meeting the VP
jokes.punchline: Math major giving mathematically impossible percentage
jokes.explanation: Good character introduction showing Craig's tech-bro obliviousness to basic logic despite his math background.

jokes.text: She's our 'financy Nancy,' but she's cool, sometimes. - I can be cool sometimes.
jokes.setup: Craig introducing Melissa with condescending nickname
jokes.punchline: Melissa's defensive response about her coolness
jokes.explanation: Good workplace dynamics humor showing the condescension and insecurity in tech culture.

jokes.text: So pretty. The museums are too big. They could be smaller.
jokes.setup: Craig asked to talk about France
jokes.punchline: His bizarrely simplistic take on French culture
jokes.explanation: Perfect tech-bro reductionism applied to culture. The suggestion that museums 'could be smaller' is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: If it's a phone and watch, just call it a 'Fotch.' Or a 'Wone.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of the 'Smartch' name
jokes.punchline: Selina's equally bad alternative portmanteaus
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina trying to contribute but missing the mark. Her alternatives are appropriately bad.

jokes.text: If you wanted it to bomb.
jokes.setup: Selina suggests 'Wone' as a name
jokes.punchline: Craig's blunt assessment of her naming skills
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Craig's oblivious rudeness to the Vice President.

jokes.text: I feel like we're sawing a tree or something. Now we're milking a cow.
jokes.setup: Trying to get the smart watch to work through handshakes
jokes.punchline: Selina's descriptions of their increasingly ridiculous handshaking attempts
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy with Selina's characteristic blunt observations about absurd situations.

jokes.text: We have a saying here at Clovis... 'Dare to fail.' - Then that's a job well done.
jokes.setup: Craig offers corporate motto after device failure
jokes.punchline: Selina sarcastically applies it to their obvious failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline where Selina turns corporate speak against itself

jokes.text: We have a saying here at Clovis... 'Dare to fail.' - Then that's a job well done.
jokes.setup: Craig explaining company philosophy after tech failure
jokes.punchline: Selina's sarcastic assessment that they've succeeded at failing
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup-punchline with perfect Selina sarcasm. The logic is ironclad and the economy is perfect.

jokes.text: Hey, do you think I should go with this? I'm probably going to, but if you think I shouldn't go with it, just call me back. Okay? Call me back if I shouldn't go with it.
jokes.setup: Jonah leaving voicemail about publishing story
jokes.punchline: His circular logic creating impossible conditions for being stopped
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah character moment showing his fake consultation while ensuring he gets his way. The logic trap is beautifully constructed.

jokes.text: The Smartch learns quickly. It's like it has an Asian mother.
jokes.setup: Gary trying to demonstrate the smart watch
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate racial stereotype about learning
jokes.explanation: Shows Gary's tendency toward inappropriate comments, but the joke itself relies on cheap stereotyping rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Now I'm getting fucked harder that way.
jokes.setup: Porn parody appearing on screen instead of intended website
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good Selina character moment showing her blunt assessment of political damage in crude terms.

jokes.text: Technically, becoming vice president could count as making it.
jokes.setup: Comment about porn parody being sign of 'making it'
jokes.punchline: Amy's dry observation about the VP already having achieved something
jokes.explanation: Good Amy deadpan moment pointing out the obvious achievement being overlooked.

jokes.text: Yeah, I can't take it from them, too. You know? A girl gets tired.
jokes.setup: Discussion of multiple scandals hitting Selina
jokes.punchline: Her weary admission of being overwhelmed by constant attacks
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's rare moment of vulnerability expressed in her typical crude way.

jokes.text: We see ourselves as very much post-tax. You don't follow politics, I thought. Or you do now follow politics?
jokes.setup: Craig reveals tax avoidance desires
jokes.punchline: Selina catches his contradiction about not following politics
jokes.explanation: Selina catches Craig in his obvious contradiction about political awareness

jokes.text: People want to work with us more than they want to be paid. That's a given.
jokes.setup: Question about paying content providers
jokes.punchline: Craig's delusional belief that people prefer working for free
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of tech company entitlement and exploitation dressed up as partnership. Perfect Craig character moment.

jokes.text: If that plan works, you've changed humanity.
jokes.setup: Craig's claim about people preferring to work for free
jokes.punchline: Amy's sarcastic assessment of the revolutionary nature of free labor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy deadpan response highlighting the absurdity of Craig's worldview with surgical precision.

jokes.text: Fair enough. Four to six. He said he's a popular guy, so... - I didn't say that. - Yes, he definitely said that.
jokes.setup: Craig deciding how much to pay for Jonah's website
jokes.punchline: Craig's complete misremembering/fabrication of Dan's comment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Craig's selective hearing and tendency to create his own reality.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. You see these brains? See why I'm pattin' 'em? Because they are made of solid platinum.
jokes.setup: Jonah celebrating the potential sale
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous self-congratulation with brain-patting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment with characteristic over-the-top self-praise and physical comedy.

jokes.text: Jonah with money. God Almighty. It's like if Hitler could fly.
jokes.setup: Amy contemplates the horror of wealthy Jonah
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to an airborne Nazi leader
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant dark comedy metaphor - vivid, shocking, and perfectly captures the escalated threat level

jokes.text: Fuck you, Kent Davison. Fuck you, Mrs. Gravestock from the third grade. Fuck you, stepdad one and stepdad three.
jokes.setup: Jonah's victory lap over potential wealth
jokes.punchline: His specific list of people who doubted him, including childhood grudges
jokes.explanation: Excellent character development showing Jonah's deep insecurities and specific grudges. The escalation from current enemies to childhood teacher is perfect.

jokes.text: I think I just had a money-gasm.
jokes.setup: Jonah overwhelmed by potential wealth
jokes.punchline: His crude portmanteau for financial excitement
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah crude wordplay that shows character but isn't particularly clever writing.

jokes.text: It's like if Hitler could fly.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Jonah having money
jokes.punchline: Comparing wealthy Jonah to airborne Hitler
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy with perfect Amy delivery. The Hitler comparison is shocking but the 'could fly' addition makes it absurdist rather than just offensive. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: After taxes, it's like 500,000. It's nothing. What are you gonna do with that?
jokes.setup: Dan calculating Jonah's potential earnings
jokes.punchline: Dismissing half a million dollars as 'nothing'
jokes.explanation: Good Dan character moment showing his casual dismissal of amounts that would be life-changing to normal people.

jokes.text: 'Cause when you do that to the vice president, this is what happens!
jokes.setup: Gary apparently hurting Selina during massage demonstration
jokes.punchline: Selina's dramatic reaction to physical pain
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment showing Selina's dramatic reactions and Gary's well-meaning incompetence.

jokes.text: like you're C-3PO with a big, brass, shiny erection
jokes.setup: Selina criticizing Amy's attitude around tech
jokes.punchline: Her specific and crude Star Wars metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Selina insult combining pop culture reference with crude imagery. The specificity makes it exceptionally funny and quotable.

jokes.text: They have a bathroom here, or do they put their turds up in the cloud?
jokes.setup: Selina needing bathroom at tech company
jokes.punchline: Her crude joke about cloud storage extending to bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Great Selina character line combining tech satire with her characteristic crudeness. The absurd extension of cloud storage is perfect.

jokes.text: I'm flattered, but the sheer positivity of this place would break me.
jokes.setup: Amy being offered a job at Clovis
jokes.punchline: Her honest assessment that optimism would destroy her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy character moment showing her self-awareness about her cynical nature. The honesty is both funny and touching.

jokes.text: Go fuck yourself. Sorry, that's the opposite of what I meant to say.
jokes.setup: Amy responding to job offer
jokes.punchline: Her immediate crude rejection followed by polite correction
jokes.explanation: Excellent Amy character moment showing her reflexive hostility followed by rare moment of politeness. Great structure and timing.

jokes.text: It was a masseuse. - That makes total sense now. - Who did you think it was?
jokes.setup: Gary clarifying about the man in Selina's room
jokes.punchline: Kent's obvious relief and Gary's oblivious follow-up
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment playing on the implications of men visiting female politicians privately.

jokes.text: Not to split hairs or anything, but it was a man. So, technically, it's a masseur, not a masseuse.
jokes.setup: Gary saying 'masseuse' about male massage therapist
jokes.punchline: Kent's pedantic correction missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent character moment showing his focus on technical accuracy while missing social dynamics.

jokes.text: some people call me 'Madam Vice President.' But you can call me whatever you want, Craig. - 'Cray-eeg.'
jokes.setup: Selina being gracious about titles
jokes.punchline: Craig only caring about correcting his own name pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the pronunciation gag showing Craig's self-absorption even when meeting the VP.

jokes.text: the words 'Danny Chung' and 'torture'... they don't belong in the same sentence. They don't. 'Danny Chung'? 'Torture'? Come on.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to defend Chung from torture allegations
jokes.punchline: Her repeatedly saying the words together while claiming they don't go together
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire showing how attempts to deny connections often reinforce them. Perfect Selina obliviousness to her own self-defeating strategy.

jokes.text: And I wonder what magician got that little story out there?
jokes.setup: Craig observes how Selina's defense backfired
jokes.punchline: His knowing implication about who planted the story while addressing the audience
jokes.explanation: Craig's sly acknowledgment of the political manipulation at work

jokes.text: I can buy a thousand Amys.
jokes.setup: Craig dismissing Amy after she rejects working there
jokes.punchline: His casual assumption that talent is simply purchasable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Craig character moment showing tech bro arrogance and reduction of people to commodities.

jokes.text: You take that chicken soup and you shove it up your soul.
jokes.setup: Jonah angry about losing the deal
jokes.punchline: His mangled attempt at a threatening insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah character moment showing his inability to insult properly even when genuinely angry.

jokes.text: I got some meat you can put in your mouth. You fuckin' asshole! Hey, you want a complimentary soda?
jokes.setup: Jonah confronts Dan in pizza delivery costume
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo followed by profanity then return to fake customer service
jokes.explanation: Jonah's anger management issues showing through his ridiculous revenge costume

jokes.text: You fucked me, Dan. You fucked me with your face.
jokes.setup: Jonah confronting Dan about sabotaging his deal
jokes.punchline: His bizarre specification of how Dan betrayed him
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Jonah insult with perfect absurdist logic. 'Fucked me with your face' is both crude and weirdly specific in the best way.

jokes.text: In the ladies' restroom, they have some sort of an Internet toilet. I couldn't figure out how to flush it. It's got all these buttons that freak me out
jokes.setup: Selina describing her bathroom experience
jokes.punchline: Her fear and confusion about high-tech toilet features
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's relationship with technology and her willingness to delegate even bathroom problems.
